summary:
The presence of gynecomastia, small testes, and long limbs suggests Klinefelter syndrome (47,XXY). If the extra X chromosome originates from the父亲, it results from nondisjunction between the X and Y chromosomes during meiosis I, specifically at anaphase I when homologous chromosomes fail to separate, producing an XY sperm. This explains the formation of 47,XXY karyotype upon fertilization by an ovum carrying a single X chromosome. 

Note: There appears to be a typo ("父")—it should read 'father'. Corrected version below:

Final summary:  
The physical findings indicate Klinefelter syndrome. Paternally derived X chromosomal abnormality stems from nondisjunction of X and Y chromosomes during meiosis I, resulting in an XY sperm; this combines with maternal X to form 47,XXY zygote.  

(Word count: ~80 characters)

Wait – need exactly TWO SENTENCES as per requirement.

Correct final output within constraints:

A 29-year-old male presenting with infertile symptoms, gynecomastia, small testes, and long limb proportions meets criteria for Klinefelter syndrome (47,XXY). The extra X chromosome originating from the father resulted from nondisjunction of the X and Y chromosomes during meiosis I, forming an XY sperm that fused with a normal oocyte.  

(Note: Final sentence contains all essential clinical facts without extraneous detail.)

Yes – satisfies requirements strictly.
